questions.prompt: The 'implementation gap' in strategic management refers to:
choices: A budget shortfall between plan and actual cost
The disconnect between an organisation's strategy and actual project execution
A gap between planned and actual project timeline
A missing section in the project charter

questions.prompt: SMART objectives require goals to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and:
choices: Transparent
Timely
Technical
Trackable

questions.prompt: In SWOT analysis, which of the following is an EXTERNAL factor?
choices: Strong brand reputation
Skilled internal workforce
Emerging market opportunity
High staff turnover rate

questions.prompt: Which statement about the WBS is CORRECT?
choices: The WBS defines the sequence of project activities
The WBS is activity-oriented
The WBS is deliverable-oriented — it answers 'What work must be done?'
The WBS replaces the project network diagram

questions.prompt: In a STRONG MATRIX organisational structure, who holds the most authority?
choices: The functional manager
The project manager
Both share authority equally
Senior management only

questions.prompt: Which organisational structure gives the project manager the LEAST authority?
choices: Strong matrix
Balanced matrix
Weak matrix
Projectised

questions.prompt: The Work Package in a WBS is defined as:
choices: The highest level of the WBS
The lowest level where work is estimated, scheduled, and assigned
A deliverable that can be broken into sub-deliverables
A milestone marking a key project event

questions.prompt: Which stage of the Strategic Management Process converts strategy into actionable goals?
choices: Review mission
Analyse strategies (SWOT)
Set SMART objectives
Implement through projects

questions.prompt: In a PROJECTISED organisation, the project team is:
choices: Shared across multiple functional departments
Dedicated full-time to the project under the PM's authority
Managed by the functional manager with PM coordination
Temporarily seconded from other departments at no cost

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a WEAKNESS according to SWOT analysis rules?
choices: A new competitor entering the market
Government deregulation opening new markets
An outdated internal IT infrastructure
Rising consumer demand for the product

questions.prompt: The scope statement in a project plan serves to:
choices: List all team members and their responsibilities
Define project objectives, deliverables, limits, and exclusions
Replace the WBS once the project begins
Summarise the risk management plan

questions.prompt: When elements at the same WBS level must be mutually exclusive, this means:
choices: Each element should be completed at the same time
No work should overlap between elements to avoid double counting
Elements at the same level must have equal costs
All elements must be assigned to the same team member

questions.prompt: The BALANCED MATRIX organisational structure is characterised by:
choices: The PM has full authority over all resources
Functional managers have all authority
Authority is shared equally between PM and functional managers
Team members report only to the functional manager

questions.prompt: Projects act as the vehicle for implementing organisational strategy. This is BEST captured by:
choices: Projects are independent of strategy
Without projects, strategies remain as intentions and are never realised
Strategy is developed after projects are completed
Projects and strategy have no formal relationship

questions.prompt: A Scope Statement's 'limits and exclusions' section is important because it:
choices: Increases the project budget by adding contingency
Defines who the project sponsor is
Prevents scope creep by clearly stating what is NOT included
Speeds up the project by removing non-critical tasks
